column2: Definition

Inclusive Recovery Indicators: For each of the following indicators, we've included "raw score," "z-score," and "rank" for each year of 1980, 1990, 2000, and 2013  (based on 2011-2015 5-year estimates). Raw score refers to the absolute score of each city on that measure. Z-score refers to the relative score of each city on that measure in comparison to the full 274 city sample, with 0 as the mean, and 1 as the standard deviation. Rank refers to the relative rank, 1-274 with 1 being best and 274 being worst amongst the full city sample in that particular year. For our three indexes, only a z-score and rank are given (as the raw score is itself a z-score).
Percentage change in number of people in the labor force employed from 10 years prior (13 years for 2013)
column3: (# people employed in current decade- # people employed in previous decade)/# people employed in previous decade

column2: Median family income in the past 12 months
Percentage of labor force not currently employed or actively seeking employment (excluding military)
Percentage of housing units that are for sale or rented/sold but unoccupied
Average of the z-scores of all 4 economic health indicators
column3: (Employment Change + Median Family Income + Unemployment Rate + Vacancy Rate)/4

column2: Rank-order information theory index on a scale of 0 to 1
column3: Computation of segregation between families above and below each income distribution bucket at the census tract level. These are then averaged (weighted by income comparative to the median income) to construct the city-level measure.

column2: Percentage of 16-19-year-olds who are not enrolled in school and are not high school graduates
column3: # not enrolled or school and non-HS graduates aged 16-19/# total population aged 16-19

column2: Percentage of renters who pay 35% or more of their income in rent
column3: # renters paying over 35% of their income on rent/total renters

column2: Percentage of families that are below the poverty line with householder working full-time
column3: # families below the poverty level with a householder or spouse that worked full-time year-round in the past 12 months/# families

column2: Average of the z-scores of all 4 economic inclusion indicators
column3: (Income Segregation + Percent 16-19-Year-Olds Not in School + Rent Burden + Working Poor)/4

column2: Person of color/white (non-Hispanic) dissimilarity index
column3: (1/2) * ((# people of color in census tract /# people of color in city) Đ (# non-Hispanic white in census tract/ # non-Hispanic white in city))

column2: Percentage person of color population (over age 25) with a high school degree or more minus the percentage white population (over age 25) with high school degree or more
column3: (# white HS graduates/# total HS graduates) - (# people of color HS graduates/# total HS graduates)

column2: Percentage of white households that own a home minus the percentage of person of color households that own a home
column3: (#non-Hispanic white in owner-occupied units/# non-Hispanic white in occupied units) - (# people of color in owner-occupied units/#people of color in occupied units)

column2: Poverty rate for person of color population minus the poverty rate for person of color population
column3: (# people of color below the poverty line/# people of color) Đ (# white below the poverty line/# white)

column2: Percentage of population made up by people of color
Average of the z-scores of all 5 racial  inclusion indicators
column3: (Racial Segregation + Racial Education Gap + Homeownership Gap + Poverty Gap + Percentage of color)/5

column2: Average of the z-scores of all 9 economic inclusion and racial  inclusion indicators
column3: (Income Segregation + Percent 16-19-Year-Olds Not in School + Rent Burden + Working Poor + Racial Segregation + Racial Education Gap + Homeownership Gap + Poverty Gap + Percentage of color)/9
